_comment: Project-level permission policy for Claude Code.
This file is checked into version control and applies to every
contributor who opens this repository. Personal overrides go in
.claude/settings.local.json, which is gitignored.
Notes on specific rules:
* The Bash matcher uses shell-style glob patterns where `*`
matches any sequence of characters (including spaces and
flags). Deny entries are checked first and win on overlap.
* `git add -A` / `git add --all` are denied to force
path-scoped staging, so a commit's working set stays
visible and reviewable. `git commit -a` / `--all` is denied
for the same reason.
* Branch discipline: main and master are protected. The agent
is expected to create a topic branch before editing (see
CLAUDE.md §9 and docs/agent/how-to-start-a-new-feature.md).
Concretely:
git switch -c feat/foo      -> allowed (new branch)
git checkout -b fix/bar     -> allowed (new branch)
git switch feat/foo         -> allowed (existing topic)
git switch main             -> DENIED
git checkout main           -> DENIED
git switch master           -> DENIED
git rebase main             -> allowed (rebases *onto* main,
does not mutate main)
git push origin feat/foo    -> allowed
git push origin main        -> DENIED
git push origin HEAD:main   -> DENIED (by *:main* rule)
git push --force            -> DENIED
The deny rules use `main*` / `master*` globs, which also
cover `main ` followed by flags (e.g. `git checkout main --`).
They would also deny a branch literally named `mainstream-*`;
override locally in .claude/settings.local.json if needed.
* `WebFetch` is denied; see
docs/agent/external-information.md for what to do when
external information is genuinely needed, and
docs/agent/verifying-current-practice.md for how to check
library versions and API shapes without it.
Adjust the allow/deny lists to match your project's tooling.
Local-only enforcement is only half the story: pair this with
a GitHub branch-protection rule on main that requires PRs and
blocks direct pushes. See README 'Getting started'.
